meta.title: Run Faster
meta.description: Advanced character options for Shadowrun 5th Edition, including new metatypes, qualities, creation methods, lifestyles, contacts, and more.

metatypes.name: Gnome
metatypes.description: Small, magically resistant dwarves known for their youthful appearance and arcane nullifying abilities.

metatypes.name: Hanuman
metatypes.description: Simian dwarves from the Indian subcontinent with prehensile tails and monkey-like features.

metatypes.name: Koborokuru
metatypes.description: Japanese dwarves known for their speed and unusual body hair patterns.

metatypes.name: Menehune
metatypes.description: Hawaiian dwarves adapted to aquatic environments with exceptional swimming abilities.

metatypes.name: Dryad
metatypes.description: Elf variants with an innate connection to nature, possessing glamour and symbiotic plant bonds.

metatypes.name: Nocturna
metatypes.description: Nocturnal elves covered in colored fur, with enhanced hearing and aversion to sunlight.

metatypes.name: Wakyambi
metatypes.description: Exceptionally tall, slender African elves known for their elongated limbs and speed.

metatypes.name: Xapiri Thëpë
metatypes.description: Amazonian elves who photosynthesize, sensitive to pollutants but deeply connected to nature.

metatypes.name: Nartaki
metatypes.description: Multi-armed humans from South Asia, possessing an extra pair of arms and striking skin coloring.

metatypes.name: Hobgoblin
metatypes.description: European ork variants with pronounced fangs, vivid eyes, and a vindictive temperament.

metatypes.name: Ogre
metatypes.description: Massive ork variants with iron stomachs and exceptional physical strength.

metatypes.name: Oni
metatypes.description: Japanese ork variants with striking skin pigmentation and balanced physical attributes.

metatypes.name: Satyr
metatypes.description: Goat-legged ork variants known for their agility and social charm.

metatypes.name: Cyclopean
metatypes.description: One-eyed troll variants with massive frames and extraordinary strength.

metatypes.name: Fomorian
metatypes.description: Celtic troll variants with arcane resistance and formidable physical power.

metatypes.name: Giant
metatypes.description: Bark-skinned troll variants of enormous size with natural dermal armor.

metatypes.name: Minotaur
metatypes.description: Bull-horned troll variants with devastating gore attacks and immense body mass.

metatypes.name: Centaur
metatypes.description: Four-legged metasapients with innate magical abilities, combining equine and humanoid forms.

metatypes.name: Naga
metatypes.description: Serpentine metasapients with natural armor, venom, and dual-natured perception.

metatypes.name: Pixie
metatypes.description: Tiny winged metasapients with natural concealment abilities but limited education.

metatypes.name: Sasquatch
metatypes.description: Massive primate metasapients with mimicry abilities and dual-natured awareness.

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Bovine)
metatypes.description: Awakened bovine shapeshifters capable of assuming metahuman form, known for their physical endurance and formidable strength.

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Canine)
metatypes.description: Awakened canine shapeshifters with keen senses and pack instincts, equally at home in the wilds or the urban sprawl.

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Equine)
metatypes.description: Awakened equine shapeshifters prized for their raw power and speed, often found ranging across the NAN territories.

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Falconine)
metatypes.description: Awakened raptor shapeshifters with extraordinary eyesight and devastating aerial strikes, among the rarest of their kind.
racialTraits: Natural Weapon (Bite: DV (STR +2)P, AP -1, Reach -1; Talons: DV (STR)P, AP -)

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Leonine)
metatypes.description: Awakened great cat shapeshifters embodying predatory grace and raw power, feared across the African and Asian wilderness.
racialTraits: Natural Weapon (Bite: DV (STR +1)P, AP -1; Claws: DV (STR +1)P, AP -1, +1 Reach)

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Lupine)
metatypes.description: Awakened wolf shapeshifters with heightened senses and fierce loyalty, thriving in both wilderness packs and shadowrunner teams.

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Pantherine)
metatypes.description: Awakened panther shapeshifters combining stealth and cunning with razor-sharp instincts, favored as infiltrators in the shadows.
racialTraits: Natural Weapon (Bite: DV (STR +2)P, AP -3; Claws: DV (STR +1)P, AP -)

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Tigrine)
metatypes.description: Awakened tiger shapeshifters possessing devastating strength and predatory intelligence, among the most dangerous shifters alive.
racialTraits: Natural Weapon (Bite: DV (STR +2)P, AP -2; Claws: DV (STR +1)P, AP -1, +1 Reach)

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Ursine)
metatypes.description: Awakened bear shapeshifters of immense physical power and resilience, as comfortable in the frozen barrens as in a brawl.
racialTraits: Natural Weapon (Bite: DV (STR +2)P, AP -2; Claws: DV (STR +3)P, AP -1, +1 Reach)

metatypes.name: Shapeshifter (Vulpine)
metatypes.description: Awakened fox shapeshifters renowned for their cunning and adaptability, often blending seamlessly into metahuman society.

negative.summary: Soft elongated skull deforms easily; -1 die to resist knockdown and called shots to the head deal +1 DV.

negative.name: Cold-Blooded
negative.summary: Body temperature depends on environment; -1 to all actions in cold conditions, lethargy below 5C.

negative.name: Critter Spook
negative.summary: Animals are instinctively hostile or fearful; -2 dice to Animal Handling Tests, animals may flee or attack.

negative.name: Cyclopean Eye
negative.summary: Single large central eye lacks depth perception; -1 die to ranged attacks and distance estimation.

negative.name: Deformity (Picasso)
negative.summary: Facial features are asymmetric and rearranged; -2 dice to social tests with non-changelings.

negative.name: Deformity (Quasimodo)
negative.summary: Severe skeletal deformity with hunched back; -3 dice to social tests, -1 AGI, halved movement.

negative.name: Feathers (Negative)
negative.summary: Body covered in obvious feathers that cannot be hidden, causing social stigma without protective benefit.

negative.name: Impaired Attribute
negative.summary: Maximum for one attribute is permanently reduced by 1 due to metagenic mutation.

negative.name: Insectoid Features
negative.summary: Compound eyes, mandibles, or chitinous patches give an insect-like appearance; -2 social dice with non-changelings.

negative.name: Mood Hair
negative.summary: Hair changes color based on emotional state, making it impossible to hide feelings; -1 to Con Tests.

negative.name: Neoteny
negative.summary: Retain juvenile physical features permanently; treated as a child socially, -2 to Leadership and Intimidation.

negative.name: Nocturnal
negative.summary: Body is adapted to nighttime activity; -1 to all actions in bright light or during daytime hours.

negative.name: Progeria
negative.summary: Accelerated aging causes premature physical decline; -1 BOD and -1 STR, aged appearance.

negative.name: Scales (Negative)
negative.summary: Obvious reptilian scales cover the body without armor benefit, causing social stigma.

negative.name: Scent Glands
negative.summary: Produce strong uncontrollable musk; -2 dice to social tests in close proximity and easier to track by scent.

negative.name: Slow Healer
negative.summary: Natural healing time is doubled; magical healing effects are reduced by 1 hit.

negative.name: Striking Skin Pigmentation
negative.summary: Skin is an unnatural vivid color (blue, green, red, etc.) impossible to conceal without disguise.

negative.name: Stubby Arms
negative.summary: Arms are disproportionately short; -1 Reach, -2 dice to tasks requiring long arm reach.

negative.name: Symbiosis
negative.summary: Dependent on a specific environmental condition or substance; take 1 box of Stun per day without it.

negative.name: Third Eye
negative.summary: Vestigial third eye on forehead is non-functional but highly visible, causing social stigma.

negative.name: Unusual Hair
negative.summary: Hair is replaced by quills, tentacles, or other non-hair growths that are impossible to disguise.

negative.name: Vestigial Tail
negative.summary: Non-functional tail that gets in the way; -1 die to Gymnastics and must accommodate in clothing and seating.

creationMethods.name: Sum to Ten
creationMethods.description: A flexible variant of the Priority system. Assign priority levels A-E to five categories, where A=4, B=3, C=2, D=1, E=0 points. Levels may be repeated, but the total must equal exactly 10.

steps.title: Spells
steps.description: Select spells if you are a magician or mystic adept.

steps.title: Rituals
steps.description: Learn ritual spellcasting if your magical path includes it.

steps.title: Adept Powers
steps.description: Select adept powers using your Power Points (equal to Magic for Adepts).

steps.title: Build Contacts
steps.description: Build your network of contacts using free Karma based on Charisma.

steps.title: Augmentations
steps.description: Install cyberware and bioware. Essence loss affects Magic rating.

steps.title: Matrix Programs
steps.description: Select Matrix programs for your cyberdeck

steps.title: Spend Karma
steps.description: Spend remaining Karma on spells, complex forms, or stat improvements. Max 7 Karma carryover.

steps.title: Identities & SINs
steps.description: Create identities with SINs (fake or real) and licenses. Each character must have at least one identity.

budgets.label: Contact Karma
budgets.description: Free Karma for contacts = CHA x 3

constraints.description: Priority point values (A=4, B=3, C=2, D=1, E=0) must total exactly 10.
constraints.errorMessage: Priority points must total exactly 10.

constraints.description: Only one attribute can be at natural maximum at creation.
constraints.errorMessage: Only one attribute can be at its natural maximum.

constraints.description: Maximum 25 Karma in Positive Qualities.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot spend more than 25 Karma on positive qualities.

constraints.description: Maximum 25 Karma from Negative Qualities.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot gain more than 25 Karma from negative qualities.

constraints.description: Skills capped at 6 at creation (7 with Aptitude).
constraints.errorMessage: Skill rating cannot exceed 6 at creation (7 with Aptitude).

constraints.description: Edge starts at metatype value. Magic/Resonance start at priority value (0 for mundane).
constraints.errorMessage: Special attributes must meet minimum requirements.

constraints.description: Gear availability cannot exceed 12 at creation.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot purchase gear with availability higher than 12.

constraints.description: Essence cannot drop below 0.
constraints.errorMessage: Essence cannot drop below 0.

creationMethods.name: Point Buy
creationMethods.description: A fully karma-based creation system with 800 Karma. Purchase metatype, attributes, skills, qualities, gear, and more using advancement costs. Offers maximum flexibility at the cost of more time-consuming creation.

steps.title: Select Metatype
steps.description: Choose your metatype. Cost is deducted from your 800 Karma budget.

steps.title: Magic/Resonance
steps.description: Purchase a magical or resonance quality to gain access to Magic or Resonance attributes.

steps.title: Allocate Attributes
steps.description: Raise attributes from metatype minimums using advancement costs (New Rating × 5 Karma).

steps.title: Allocate Skills
steps.description: Purchase skills using advancement costs (New Rating × 2 Karma for active, × 5 for groups, × 1 for knowledge).

steps.title: Select Qualities
steps.description: Purchase positive qualities and take negative qualities (refunds Karma).

steps.title: Spells
steps.description: Select spells if you are a magician or mystic adept (5 Karma each).

steps.title: Rituals
steps.description: Learn ritual spellcasting if your magical path includes it.

steps.title: Adept Powers
steps.description: Select adept powers using your Power Points (equal to Magic for Adepts; Mystic Adepts buy power points at 5 Karma each).

steps.title: Build Contacts
steps.description: Build your network of contacts using Karma (Connection + Loyalty per contact).

steps.title: Augmentations
steps.description: Install cyberware and bioware. Essence loss affects Magic rating. Purchased with nuyen from gear karma.

steps.title: Purchase Gear
steps.description: Purchase gear using nuyen (1 Karma = 2,000¥, max 200 Karma on gear).

steps.title: Matrix Programs
steps.description: Select Matrix programs for your cyberdeck.

steps.title: Identities & SINs
steps.description: Create identities with SINs (fake or real) and licenses.

steps.title: Review & Finalize
steps.description: Review your character. All 800 Karma must be spent — no leftover Karma allowed.

budgets.label: Karma
budgets.description: Starting Karma budget for Point Buy creation

constraints.description: Total Karma spent cannot exceed 800. Gear karma capped at 200.
constraints.errorMessage: Karma budget exceeded (800 Karma total, 200 max on gear).

constraints.description: Only one Physical or Mental attribute can be at natural maximum at creation. Special attributes (Edge, Magic, Resonance) are exempt.
constraints.errorMessage: Only one Physical or Mental attribute can be at its natural maximum.

constraints.description: Maximum 25 Karma in Positive Qualities.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot spend more than 25 Karma on positive qualities.

constraints.description: Maximum 25 Karma from Negative Qualities.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot gain more than 25 Karma from negative qualities.

constraints.description: Skills capped at 6 at creation (7 with Aptitude).
constraints.errorMessage: Skill rating cannot exceed 6 at creation (7 with Aptitude).

constraints.description: Gear availability cannot exceed 12 at creation.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot purchase gear with availability higher than 12.

constraints.description: Essence cannot drop below 0.
constraints.errorMessage: Essence cannot drop below 0.

constraints.description: All Karma must be spent — no leftover Karma allowed.
constraints.errorMessage: Leftover Karma cannot be carried over from Point Buy creation.

creationMethods.name: Life Modules
creationMethods.description: A life-path system where characters progress through nationality, formative years, teen years, further education, and real-life career modules. Each module provides attributes, skills, qualities, and knowledge. Starting budget is 750 Karma.

steps.title: Select Metatype
steps.description: Choose your metatype. Metatype costs Karma from your 750 budget.

steps.title: Magic/Resonance
steps.description: Purchase a magical or resonance quality (same costs as Point Buy: Adept 20K, Magician 30K, etc.).

steps.title: Nationality
steps.description: Select your nationality and regional origin. Provides language skills, regional knowledge, and qualities. (15 Karma)

steps.title: Formative Years
steps.description: Select your childhood upbringing (age to 10). Provides attribute points, skills, and qualities. (40 Karma)

steps.title: Teen Years
steps.description: Select your teenage experience (age to 17). Provides skills, knowledge, and qualities. (50 Karma)

steps.title: Further Education
steps.description: Optionally attend an institution and select a discipline. Costs vary by institution (40-115 Karma).

steps.title: Real Life
steps.description: Select career modules (100 Karma each, 4 years). Multiple allowed but no repeats.

steps.title: Tours of Duty
steps.description: Select military tours of duty (100 Karma each, 5 years). Multiple allowed but no repeats.

steps.title: Allocate Attributes
steps.description: Spend remaining Karma to raise attributes (New Rating × 5 Karma).

steps.title: Allocate Skills
steps.description: Spend remaining Karma on skills. Active skill max 7, knowledge skill max 9.

steps.title: Select Qualities
steps.description: Purchase positive qualities or buy off negative qualities gained from modules.

steps.title: Build Contacts
steps.description: Build contacts using Karma (in addition to any granted by modules).

steps.title: Purchase Gear
steps.description: Purchase gear using nuyen (1 Karma = 2,000¥, max 200 Karma on gear).

steps.title: Review & Finalize
steps.description: Review your character. Remaining Karma can be kept (unlike Point Buy).

budgets.label: Karma
budgets.description: Starting Karma budget for Life Modules creation (Run Faster p. 65)

constraints.description: Total Karma spent on modules and purchases cannot exceed 750.
constraints.errorMessage: Karma budget exceeded (750 Karma total, 200 max on gear).

constraints.description: Active skills capped at 7, knowledge skills capped at 9.
constraints.errorMessage: Skill rating exceeds Life Modules creation cap.

constraints.description: Only one Physical or Mental attribute can be at natural maximum at creation.
constraints.errorMessage: Only one Physical or Mental attribute can be at its natural maximum.

constraints.description: Maximum 25 Karma in Positive Qualities.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot spend more than 25 Karma on positive qualities.

constraints.description: Maximum 25 Karma from Negative Qualities after all modules.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot gain more than 25 Karma from negative qualities after all modules.

constraints.description: Gear availability cannot exceed 12 at creation.
constraints.errorMessage: Cannot purchase gear with availability higher than 12.

constraints.description: Essence cannot drop below 0.
constraints.errorMessage: Essence cannot drop below 0.

lifestyles.name: Bolt Hole
lifestyles.description: A prearranged, cash-only space to hide in when things go bad. Not a permanent residence — more like a cargo crate with food rations and a chemical toilet. Assets can be purchased but not services or outings. Only has a monthly cost when used.

lifestyles.name: Street
lifestyles.description: Living on the street with no fixed address. Increased levels in C&N, Security, and Neighborhood cost a flat 50 nuyen per level per month instead of the standard 10% formula.

lifestyles.name: Squatter
lifestyles.description: A squat in an abandoned building or similar space.

lifestyles.name: Low
lifestyles.description: A basic apartment or small house in a rough neighborhood.

lifestyles.name: Medium
lifestyles.description: A comfortable apartment or condo in a decent neighborhood.

lifestyles.name: High
lifestyles.description: An upscale home or luxury apartment.

lifestyles.name: Luxury
lifestyles.description: The finest accommodations money can buy.

lifestyles.name: Traveler
lifestyles.description: The traveler keeps mobile and packs light. Every month the traveler stays someplace new. The player rolls 1D6 to determine the number of points allocated to the lifestyle that month. Only assets and services are available in the Entertainment category, added by the gamemaster.

lifestyles.name: Commercial
lifestyles.description: A storefront, not a proper residence. The lifestyle includes a location where the character can manufacture, fence, or repair goods. Includes permits to sell specific legal items. The character should make a Charisma + Etiquette (Corporate) [Social] test at the start of each month; each net hit reduces the monthly cost by 1,000 for that month.

entertainmentOptions.name: Armory
entertainmentOptions.description: A room dedicated to guns. Ammo and one stock firearm duplicating one that the character purchased at creation are on hand. With the purchase of a toolkit, 1 point of Accuracy or AP can be repaired per week without additional cost. A point of Reach can also be repaired in two weeks.

entertainmentOptions.name: Cleaning Service (Standard)
entertainmentOptions.description: Cleaning services include tidying up the home and cleaning/mending clothes with some semblance of privacy guarantee.

entertainmentOptions.name: Cleaning Service (Mage Sensitive)
entertainmentOptions.description: Mage-sensitive cleaning services destroy biomaterials that may be left behind on clothing or in a car/premise. Any material link items found at the character's domicile or vehicle are contaminated and cannot be used for ritual purposes.

entertainmentOptions.name: Cleaning Service (Pollution Sensitive)
entertainmentOptions.description: Pollution-sensitive cleaning services use less harsh chemicals and natural materials. This reduces Fatigue damage from environmental allergies the C&I category by 1.

entertainmentOptions.name: Discreet Cleaning Service
entertainmentOptions.description: An organization that can go to a premise and patch bullet holes, wash out blood stains, and cleanse the astral in under twenty-four hours. At the listed cost, they will handle only one body disposal a month. This service is tied to the character, so it does not end when they change lifestyles.

entertainmentOptions.name: Discreet Deliveryman
entertainmentOptions.description: A guy who can pick up and deliver any legal item (within reason) without being noticed. Has a dice pool of 14 for Sneaking Tests and an Edge Rating of 4. The deliveryman can handle takeout food service and legal items with Availability 8 or less.

entertainmentOptions.name: Candyman
entertainmentOptions.description: A discreet deliveryman who can handle items up to Availability of 16, as well as restricted and illegal items. The purchase of the item must be accomplished through normal means. Time to deliver is based on original price of the item.

entertainmentOptions.name: Garage (Car, Body 4 or less)
entertainmentOptions.description: A large covered area with enough space to park and maintain a single small vehicle (Body 4 or less). With a Shop, 1 box of damage can be repaired per week without additional cost.

entertainmentOptions.name: Garage (Car, Body 5 or greater)
entertainmentOptions.description: A large covered area with enough space to park and maintain a single large vehicle (Body 5 or greater). With a Shop, 1 box of damage can be repaired per week without additional cost.

entertainmentOptions.name: Garage (Boat)
entertainmentOptions.description: Includes space on a dock and access to launch the craft. Smaller personal watercraft can be treated the same as cars for the purposes of storage and repair in lieu of owning a dock.

entertainmentOptions.name: Garage (Airplane)
entertainmentOptions.description: Hangar space for a single airplane. With a Facility, 2 boxes of damage can be repaired per week without additional cost.

entertainmentOptions.name: Garage (Helicopter)
entertainmentOptions.description: Includes access to a helicopter pad to launch the vehicle. With a Facility, 2 boxes of damage can be repaired per week without additional cost.

entertainmentOptions.name: Greenhouse
entertainmentOptions.description: A small plot of land or a couple of pots to grow carrots, tomatoes, herbs, wheatgrass, etc. Reduce the environmental damage due to food allergies by 1.

entertainmentOptions.name: Grid Subscription
entertainmentOptions.description: A subscription to a corporate-sponsored grid for entertainment, shopping, and media. Without a grid subscription, commlinks default to the public grid with only trid shows from two seasons ago.

entertainmentOptions.name: Gym
entertainmentOptions.description: Weights, treadmill, and other fitness equipment. Reduces training time to improve physical stats by twenty percent.

entertainmentOptions.name: Indoor Arboretum
entertainmentOptions.description: A room dedicated to living vegetation. A bright spot in the astral plane for the Awakened, and a room free of pollutants for the mundane. Reduces C&N environmental damage due to pollution allergies by 1.

entertainmentOptions.name: Local Bar Patron
entertainmentOptions.description: A bar or similar habitual location that the character frequents. The lifestyle covers food and drinks from this spot. One specific location is chosen when this entertainment option is selected, and the feature can be purchased more than once to add different locations.

entertainmentOptions.name: Panic Room
entertainmentOptions.description: A reinforced three-meter by three-meter room constructed with structural material (p. 197, SR5) with a Rating 5 locking mechanism.

entertainmentOptions.name: Patron of the Arts (Rock Concerts / Sport Events)
entertainmentOptions.description: Membership privileges granting front-row seats, members-only specials, and limited suite or luxury box access at rock concerts and sport events.

entertainmentOptions.name: Patron of the Arts (Private Club Memberships)
entertainmentOptions.description: Private club membership such as Dante's Inferno (membership #77). Includes preferred member treatment and exclusive access.

entertainmentOptions.name: Patron of the Arts (Public Entertainment)
entertainmentOptions.description: Regular attendance at public entertainment venues such as the zoo, museums, and similar attractions.

entertainmentOptions.name: Patron of the Arts (Theme Parks)
entertainmentOptions.description: Annual or monthly pass to theme parks such as Virtual Disneyland and other upscale attractions.

entertainmentOptions.name: Private Room
entertainmentOptions.description: A simple empty two-meter-by-two-meter space built out of the view of passersby. Provides concealability (-4 dice pool penalty for Perception Tests to notice a person in the room from the outside) and full cover.

entertainmentOptions.name: Public Transportation
entertainmentOptions.description: A monthly pass to access public transportation in your region including bus, trolley, subway, ferry, or any combination. Purchased once per city.

entertainmentOptions.name: Railway Pass
entertainmentOptions.description: A monthly pass to use the light rail or mag-lev train to travel between two relatively close sprawls. Includes border-crossing fees when relevant. Purchased for two city locations.

entertainmentOptions.name: Shooting Range
entertainmentOptions.description: A place to shoot guns without bothering the neighbors. Reduces training time with a firearm skill by ten percent.

entertainmentOptions.name: Soy Processing Unit
entertainmentOptions.description: A SOTA set of kitchen devices. Soy, krill, and mycoprotein are architecturally printed into something that looks and tastes close to the real thing. Reduces the potential environmental damage tied to Comforts & Necessities due to food allergies by 1.

entertainmentOptions.name: Sports Court
entertainmentOptions.description: A tennis court, basketball court, driving range, bowling alley, or similar physical activity space. Reduces training time with an athletics skill by ten percent.

entertainmentOptions.name: Swimming Pool
entertainmentOptions.description: A swimming pool for relaxation and exercise. Bigger than a bathtub, clean, and deep enough to use.

entertainmentOptions.name: Walk-in Freezer
entertainmentOptions.description: A chilled room mainly used for food storage, but can also store things that need to stay cold while six-foot holes are dug as more permanent resting places.

entertainmentOptions.name: Workshop/Facility
entertainmentOptions.description: Space dedicated to the character's gear-related work. Power, ventilation, shelving, etc. Combined with a Shop or Facility, gear/weapons associated with the workshop can be repaired at 1 box of damage per week without additional cost (2 boxes with a Facility).

entertainmentOptions.name: Yard
entertainmentOptions.description: A piece of outdoor ground roughly fifty to two hundred square meters. Could be a backyard, empty lot, or abandoned parking lot. Large enough to park a car, put up a small kiddy pool, or install a ritual circle. Can be purchased more than once to increase the size of the yard.

entertainmentOptions.name: Zen Den/Bat Cave
entertainmentOptions.description: A room dedicated to the hobbies of the character. The zen den provides a +1 dice pool bonus on tests involving relevant Interest Knowledge Skills when at home. The bat cave provides a +1 dice pool bonus to tests involving relevant Academic Knowledge Skills when at home (with a Kit, up to three Kits).

entertainmentOptions.name: Merchandise: Goods (Specific Item)
entertainmentOptions.description: This location will provide some sort of legal goods for sale. The item to be sold must be selected when this option is purchased. Base Availability is 3 for gear and low quality for miscellaneous items. More points can be spent to increase quality/availability (max 4 points for luxury-quality). Each point also adds 2 to the Availability of the item that can be sold on the premises.

entertainmentOptions.name: Merchandise: Pawn Shop/Thrift Store
entertainmentOptions.description: Any used legal goods can be fenced at this location. Items can be sold within a week provided that the player sells the item at fifteen percent of the normal purchase price.

entertainmentOptions.name: Merchandise: Used Goods (Specific Item)
entertainmentOptions.description: This location can be used to fence one specific item, which must be selected when this option is purchased. Reduces threshold to find a buyer by half. You can immediately sell items at twenty-five percent of the purchase price.

modifications.name: Angry Drunk Reputation
modifications.description: You have a bad reputation for causing or being in fights. Many bars have put your picture in their system or banned you completely. The character cannot have any Bar Patron or Patron of the Arts (Private Club) entertainment options attached to any lifestyle. This option may cause issues when meeting Mr. Johnson.
modifications.effects: No Bar Patron or Private Club entertainment options. +1 point to base lifestyle.

modifications.name: Corporate Owned
modifications.description: This domicile is within the jurisdiction of a corporation, such as a wageslave housing complex or arcology. Increase both the Comforts & Necessities value and limit by 1. The Security level and limit are also increased by 1. Requires a corporate or limited corporate SIN (p. 84, SR5). Reduces available points by 3; if the base lifestyle doesn't have 3 points, it can't be corporate owned.
modifications.effects: +1 C&N (value and limit), +1 Security (value and limit). Requires corporate SIN.

modifications.name: Hotel California
modifications.description: There's a limited window when someone can enter or leave the premises. The rest of the time, the character is stuck and can only use the resources the place has. The time period of lockdown is agreed upon between the gamemaster and player.
modifications.effects: Limited entry/exit windows. +1 point to base lifestyle.

modifications.name: Maid Is Out
modifications.description: This location has not been cleaned recently. Or ever. Layers of trash and mold cover most surfaces. Reduce the limit of Comforts & Necessities by 1. The Cleaning Service option cannot be purchased for this location. Increase the DV of environmental fatigue by 1 for environmental allergies.
modifications.effects: -1 C&N limit, no Cleaning Service. +1 environmental fatigue DV. +1 point to base lifestyle.

modifications.name: Not A Home
modifications.description: This location was not designed for habitation. There's no plumbing and possibly no privacy, lacking standard features such as interior walls. This can be as simple as a cargo container or rented space at a strip mall. Reduce the limit of Comforts & Necessities by 1.
modifications.effects: -1 C&N limit. +1 point to base lifestyle.

modifications.name: One Good Thing About This Place
modifications.description: No matter where the character ends up, they always have one entertainment choice guaranteed while they're there. The player chooses one entertainment asset worth either one or two points. This choice is always available at the domicile as long as the monthly cost is paid. Only available for the Traveler lifestyle.
modifications.effects: One entertainment asset always available. Traveler lifestyle only.

modifications.name: Safehouse
modifications.description: This premise is designated as a safehouse. The character has no control over what Entertainment options are factored into the place, meaning they cannot purchase Entertainment assets for a safehouse, though other assets can be added. Add 4 to the dice pool of anyone trying to track the subject while they reside there. The gamemaster decides the house rules (curfew, weapons allowed, clothing restrictions, etc.).
modifications.effects: +4 dice pool to tracking tests. No Entertainment assets. +500¥/month.

modifications.name: Safety Third
modifications.description: While there is space for a workshop or facility, it's not the best of places to work in (no ventilation, wet floor, critters). Reduce the number of 1s needed for a glitch by one. Characters must have a Shop or Facility along with a Garage or Workshop Asset. Applies to a single asset (Garage or Workshop). Can be taken more than once per asset.
modifications.effects: -1 glitch threshold in workshops. +1 point to base lifestyle.

modifications.name: Thrifty
modifications.description: So what if the shower is lukewarm and the walls are paper thin? You got a great deal on this place. Reduce the points available by 2 and the monthly cost by 1,000 nuyen. Only available for the Traveler lifestyle.

modifications.name: W Zone
modifications.description: W stands for wilderness. This location is way outside any urban area, so the grid is spotty, power is not publicly provided, and unless you have a car, the nearest public transportation is two hours away. Reduce the Neighborhood level by 1 and increase the security response by 1d6 hours.
modifications.effects: -1 Neighborhood, +1d6 hours security response. +1 point to base lifestyle.

neighborhoodZones.description: A lawless area deep in the heart of many barrens. Criminal organizations and gangs can sometimes feel uneasy here. Awakened critters and toxic spirits tend to roam wild.
Low-rent/industrial districts mostly filled with SINless squatters and the homeless. Heavy or toxic industrial facilities are included in E Zones. Security providers consider SINless non-people.

Zones between industrial and residential districts where the exposure to pollution is a little better. Run-down business districts, abandoned warehouses, forgotten tenements, landfills, freeway junctions, old cemeteries, and large parking lots.
The district has seen better days. Low-rent residences, storage facilities, small bodegas, and other similar sites.
Where the middle class lives, along with the commercial places they use and the occasional light industrial facility.
Luxury-class residences and posh commercial places. If you ask the price, you can't afford to live here.
Corporate headquarters. Skyraker condos. And the occasional private islands. Where the world's wealthiest pay to make sure they're kept safe.

archetypes.name: Arms Dealer
archetypes.description: A weapons supplier who offers firearms, ammunition, and military gear to those willing to barter.

archetypes.name: Bartender
archetypes.description: A social hub who hears everything and knows everyone in the neighborhood. Run Faster expanded version.

archetypes.name: Bodyguard
archetypes.description: A private sector security professional who provides physical protection and extra muscle.

archetypes.name: Bookie
archetypes.description: A gambling operator who takes bets, loans money, and knows the odds on everything.

archetypes.name: Border Patrol Agent
archetypes.description: A border officer who can verify SINs, provide geographic intelligence, and assist with border crossings.

archetypes.name: Bounty Hunter
archetypes.description: A professional tracker who chases down subjects for the right price.

archetypes.name: Chop Shop Mechanic
archetypes.description: A skilled vehicle mechanic who handles illegal modifications, repairs, and stolen parts.

archetypes.name: Church Pastor
archetypes.description: A community spiritual leader who provides emotional support, counseling, and sanctuary.

archetypes.name: City Official
archetypes.description: A bureaucrat who leverages political power for personal gain and can offer government resources.

archetypes.name: Club Kid
archetypes.description: A fashion-forward socialite who knows everyone on the club scene and can network with the powerful.

archetypes.name: Company Suit
archetypes.description: A corporate executive agent with access to intelligence and dirty secrets, fiercely loyal to her company.

archetypes.name: Con Fanatic
archetypes.description: An obsessive convention-goer who can help with travel arrangements and cover stories through their fandom networks.

archetypes.name: Corporate Administrator
archetypes.description: A corporate office scheduler and social hub who knows all the gossip and office politics.

archetypes.name: Corporate Wageslave
archetypes.description: A faceless corporate worker who can provide routine corporate access and an inside perspective.

archetypes.name: Coyote
archetypes.description: A smuggler who specializes in moving people across borders through tunnels, bribes, and alternative routes.

archetypes.name: Cybernetic Technician
archetypes.description: A body shop tech who repairs and installs cyberware, with discounts on used parts.

archetypes.name: Gang Boss
archetypes.description: A criminal organization leader with an army of thugs and deep underworld knowledge.

archetypes.name: Government Official
archetypes.description: A mysterious spook with government connections who trades knowledge for odd jobs.

archetypes.name: ID Manufacturer
archetypes.description: A Matrix-savvy forger who creates fake SINs and data trails for the SINless.

archetypes.name: Informant
archetypes.description: A stool pigeon with privileged inside information about criminal organizations.

archetypes.name: International Courier
archetypes.description: A trusted transporter with legitimate paperwork who moves goods across international borders.

archetypes.name: Knight Errant Dispatcher
archetypes.description: A law enforcement dispatcher who monitors security alarms and can warn runners of incoming responses.

archetypes.name: Lone Star Detective
archetypes.description: An investigator with access to criminal records, old cases, and cultivated sources.

archetypes.name: Mafia Consigliere
archetypes.description: A high-ranking Mafia advisor with legal knowledge and political influence.

archetypes.name: Media Mogul
archetypes.description: A corporate media power broker who controls what information reaches the masses.

archetypes.name: Metahuman Rights Activist
archetypes.description: A passionate activist who fights for metahuman equality and can organize protests as distractions.

archetypes.name: News Reporter
